summary_ja: 北海道釧路の警察は、飲酒のため約11時間半、幼い娘を男性の家に一人にしたとして、25歳の母親と27歳の彼氏を児童遺棄の疑いで逮捕した。日曜朝、娘はコンビニに入り「お腹が空いた」と訴え、ほぼ同時に母親から行方不明の通報があった。娘にけがはなく、両親は飲み歩き中に娘を残したことを認めた。

sentences.grammar_ja: HTMLの埋め込みタグ。ニュース本文ではなく、サイトの計測用コード。英語学習の文法対象ではない。

sentences.text: Child comes into store saying she's hungry after being left alone for 11 hours by mother, boyfriend who were out drinking
sentences.grammar_ja: 見出し文体。saying＝「〜と言いながら」、after being left alone＝受動の動名詞（放置された後）、who were out drinking＝関係代名詞節で母と彼氏を修飾。
掲載・更新時刻の表示。本文の文法ではなく、ニュースサイトのメタ情報。

sentences.text: Police in Kushiro, Hokkaido, have arrested a 25-year-old woman, a self-proclaimed tattoo artist, and her 27-year-old boyfriend, a company employee, on suspicion of child abandonment.
sentences.grammar_ja: have arrested＝現在完了（報道の定番）。on suspicion of＝「〜の疑いで」。a self-proclaimed tattoo artist＝同格の名詞句で職業を補足。

sentences.text: The two are suspected of leaving the woman’s preschool-aged daughter alone at the man's house for about 11 1/2 hours from 10 p.m. on Saturday while they were out drinking, Hokkaido Broadcasting Corp reported.
sentences.grammar_ja: are suspected of + 動名詞＝「〜した疑いがある」。while they were out drinking＝同時進行。文末の reported は報道文体の倒置（〜と報じた）。

sentences.text: The girl showed up at a convenience store at around 9:30 a.m. on Sunday.
sentences.grammar_ja: showed up＝口語的コロケーション（現れる）。at around＝「およそ〜頃」。

sentences.text: An employee called police and reported that a girl had come in alone, saying she was hungry.
sentences.grammar_ja: reported that節の中は過去完了 had come in（店に入るのが通報より前）。saying節は分詞構文で付帯状況を表す。

sentences.text: At about the same time, the girl’s mother called police and said her daughter was missing.
sentences.grammar_ja: At about the same time＝時間の対比。saidの後は間接話法で was missing（行方不明だった）。

sentences.text: The girl was unharmed and in good health.
sentences.grammar_ja: was unharmed＝受動形（けがはなかった）。in good health＝固定表現（健康状態が良好）。

sentences.text: Police said the mother and her boyfriend have admitted to leaving their daughter alone while they went out drinking together at several establishments.
sentences.grammar_ja: have admitted to + 動名詞＝「〜したことを認めた」。while they went out drinking＝その間の行為。establishments＝飲食店などの施設（複数）。

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 〜の疑いで（逮捕・捜査などの公式表現）
vocabulary.example: arrested on suspicion of child abandonment
The girl showed up at a convenience store.
The girl was unharmed and in good health.
have admitted to leaving their daughter alone

vocabulary.meaning_ja: 店・施設（ここでは飲食店）

grammar_points.title: Passive + participle (being left / saying)
grammar_points.rule: Use being + past participle after prepositions (after, by) or as a reduced clause with -ing to add background detail.
grammar_points.example: after being left alone for 11 hours ... saying she was hungry
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 「放置された後」「お腹が空いていると言いながら」のように、受動や付帯状況を簡潔に足す報道英語の型。

grammar_points.title: on suspicion of + noun
grammar_points.rule: Formal news phrase meaning someone was arrested or investigated because they are believed to have committed an offense.
grammar_points.example: arrested on suspicion of child abandonment
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 「〜の疑いで」という公式・報道向けの前置き表現。動詞は arrest, detain, question などとよく結びつく。

grammar_points.title: Reported speech with past perfect
grammar_points.rule: In news reports, use that-clauses; shift tenses back when the reporting verb is past (said/reported).
grammar_points.example: reported that a girl had come in alone
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 通報より前に起きた出来事は過去完了 had come in を使う。said/reported の後は時制を一段戻すのが基本。

grammar_points.title: while + past continuous
grammar_points.rule: while shows two actions happening during the same period, often contrasting neglect with another activity.
grammar_points.meaning_ja: 「〜している間に」という同時性。本文では飲酒中に子どもを残した状況を示す。
